conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have Alex, who is 13 years old and lives with you.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: Are you (or your partner if you have one) responsible for Alex? This means you're looking after them and paying towards their upbringing - for example, providing food, clothing, and a place to live.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex. I look after them and provide everything they need - food, clothing, a home, all of that. They live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. And just to confirm - is Alex under 16 years old, or if they're 16 or over, are they in approved education or training?

Since you mentioned Alex is 13, I assume they're under 16, but I want to make sure I have the correct information.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is 13, so they're under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about your residence status: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. One more important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex? Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Alex is 13 years old (under 16)
- You are responsible for Alex and provide for all their needs
- Alex lives with you
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim if you haven't already done so.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which means they are under 16. The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for Alex (providing food, clothing, and a home), and pays towards Alex's upbringing. The claimant lives in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the key eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant is responsible for the child, the claimant lives in the UK, and no one else is claiming for this child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for all their needs. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old, which means they are under 16. The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for Alex (providing food, clothing, and a home), and pays towards Alex's upbringing. The claimant lives in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the key eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant is responsible for the child, the claimant lives in the UK, and no one else is claiming for this child.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for all their needs. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
